protocol.embedder: intfloat/multilingual-e5-small (SAME for both systems; query:/passage: prefixes by role)
protocol.omnifuse: field-weighted BM25 (lexical) fused with e5 dense; best of {dense, rrf, union_primary} per row
protocol.note: reranker (bge-reranker-v2-m3) is CPU-infeasible here (~2 pairs/sec); this is the dense-both-sides cut

full_pipeline.name: Ko-StrategyQA
full_pipeline.note: flips the lexical-track loss; omni dense 0.7755 / union 0.7780 both beat syn-dense 0.7374

combined_conclusion.lexical_track: OmniFuse 7 wins, 1 tie, 2 losses (AutoRAG, Ko-StrategyQA) vs synaptic-FTS
combined_conclusion.full_pipeline_flips: the 2 lexical losses (AutoRAG, Ko-StrategyQA) FLIP to OmniFuse wins in the dense/full-pipeline setting
combined_conclusion.fused_vs_fused: OmniFuse wins 6/7 (HotPot x2, Allganize x2, AutoRAG, Ko-StrategyQA), loses PublicHealthQA; synaptic's own fusion underperforms its FTS on AutoRAG (0.9053 -> 0.8576)
combined_conclusion.best_single_mode_caveat: under each system's single best mode, AutoRAG flips to synaptic (FTS 0.9053 beats all fused scores); PublicHealthQA embedder-dependent (omni wins with bge-m3)
combined_conclusion.net: across lexical + dense, OmniFuse is stronger-or-equal on 8-9 of 10; contested sets = AutoRAG, PublicHealthQA (within ~0.01-0.03 MRR)
combined_conclusion.single_config_caveat: no single fusion config wins all 10 (task-dependent, Pareto) — true for synaptic too; 'best' picks the strongest of dense/rrf/union_primary per corpus
